The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents each section of the document once. I have verified that titles, headings, list items, and website URLs from all three pages of the source PDF appear exactly once in the structured JSON. There are no repeated paragraphs or sections.

The main title 'NC OneMap Update' is correctly identified as a heading (H2). Subsequent major sections 'Data Releases' and 'Tasks' are used as titles for structural subsections, which is an acceptable normalization for document organization. The hierarchy flows logically from the document title to its main content sections.
The source document does not contain any tables; it consists entirely of slide titles, text blocks, and nested lists. No table structures were incorrectly injected or corrupted.

The JSON preserves the reading order of the source PDF: Page 1 (Title/Date), Page 2 (Data Releases/List/Website), and Page 3 (Tasks/Nested Lists). The positioning of the Geoportal website info (located in a side bar on Page 2) at the end of that section's content is a standard and acceptable accessibility adjustment.

The complex nested lists on pages 2 and 3 are faithfully represented. On page 3, the multi-level nesting (Ongoing -> Infrastructure buildout -> Replicate...) is correctly captured using nested list objects within the JSON structure. The relationship between parent and child bullet points is perfectly preserved.
The URL 'data.nconemap.com' present on page 2 of the source is correctly captured as text within the paragraph content. No external links were fabricated, and no existing link-like text was omitted.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output effectively reinterprets the slide-based source PDF into a standard document format. The hierarchical organization of information—moving from the title slide to the 'Data Releases' and 'Tasks' sections—is preserved. The visual flow follows the logical sequence of the original presentation, and the change from a fixed landscape layout to a responsive portrait layout is an expected and acceptable transformation.
No tables were identified in the source PDF pages or the resulting HTML output.

On page 3 of the source PDF, the sub-list items under 'Infrastructure buildout' are marked with green checkmark icons, which visually communicate the status of those tasks. In the converted HTML output, these meaningful icons are missing and have been replaced by generic square bullet points. This loss of visual symbology results in the removal of specific status information that was present in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: On page 3, three list items under 'Infrastructure buildout' (e.g., 'Replicate data between web servers') are preceded by green checkmark icons.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the same three items are preceded by standard square bullet points, with no checkmark symbol present.

checks.a_analysis: A thorough visual comparison confirms that no content blocks, paragraphs, or lists are duplicated in the HTML output. Each piece of information from the three source slides appears exactly once in the rendered content.
